seoTitle: Shoal Bay vs Simpson — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Shoal Bay and Simpson. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Shoal Bay vs Simpson: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Shoal Bay and Simpson.
heading: Shoal Bay vs Simpson — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Shoal Bay, Northern Territory and Simpson, Northern Territory. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Shoal Bay has a lower overall climate risk score (28/100) compared to Simpson (29/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Shoal Bay or Simpson safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Shoal Bay has a flood risk score of 35/100, while Simpson scores 22/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Shoal Bay scores 27/100 for bushfire risk. Simpson scores 45/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Shoal Bay or Simpson?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Shoal Bay has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area face minimal climate value impact based on current projections.
